segments.text: After this there was a Jewish festival, so Jesus went to Jerusalem.
Now near the Sheep Gate in Jerusalem is a pool called Bethzatha in Hebrew, with five porches beside it.
Crowds of sick people were lying in these porches—those who were blind, lame, or paralyzed.
One man who was there had been sick for thirty-eight years. Jesus looked at him, knowing he had been lying there for a long time, and asked him,
Do you want to be healed?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Sir,
segments.speaker: Impotent Man

segments.text: the sick man answered,
I don't have anyone to help me get into the pool when the water is stirred. While I'm trying to get there, someone always gets in before me.
segments.speaker: The sick man

segments.text: St and up, pick up your mat, and start walking!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus told him.
Immediately the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking. Now the day that this happened was the Sabbath.
So the Jews said to the man who'd been healed, {
This is the Sabbath! It's against the law to carry a mat!
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and start walking,
segments.speaker: Healed Man

segments.text: he replied.
Who's this person who told you to carry your mat and walk?
segments.speaker: Jews

segments.text: they asked.
However, the man who'd been healed didn't know who it was, for Jesus had slipped away into the surrounding crowd.
Later on Jesus found the man in the Temple, and told him, {
Look, now you've been healed. So stop sinning or something worse may happen to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The man went and told the Jews it was Jesus who had healed him.
So the Jews started to harass Jesus because he was doing things on the Sabbath.
But Jesus told them, {
My Father is still working, and so am I.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This was why the Jews tried even harder to kill him, for not only did he break the Sabbath but also called God his Father, making himself equal with God.
So Jesus explained to them, {
I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself; he can only do what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does the Son does as well.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For the Father loves the Son, and reveals to him everything he does; and the Father will show to him even more incredible things that will completely amaze you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For just as the Father gives life to those he resurrects from the dead, in the same way the Son also gives life to those that he wants.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Father judges no one. He has given to the Son all the authority to judge,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: so that everyone may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Anyone who doesn't honor the Son doesn't honor the Father who sent him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you the truth: those who follow what I say and trust the one who sent me have eternal life. They won't be condemned, but have gone from death to life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you the truth: The time is coming—in fact it's here already—when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Just as the Father has life-giving power in himself, so has he given the Son the same life-giving power in himself.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Father also granted the authority for judgment to him, for he is the Son of man.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't be surprised at this, for the time is coming when all those in the grave will hear his voice
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and will rise again: those who have done good to the resurrection of life, and those who have done evil to the resurrection of condemnation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I can do nothing by myself. I judge based on what I'm told,  and my decision is right, for I'm not doing my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If I were to make claims about myself, such claims wouldn't be valid;
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but someone else gives evidence about me, and I know what he says about me is true.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You asked John about me, and he told the truth,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but I don't need any human endorsement. I'm explaining this to you so you can be saved.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: John was like a brightly-burning light, and you were willing to enjoy his light for a while.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But the evidence I'm giving is greater than John's. For I am doing the work that the Father gave me to do,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and this is the proof that the Father sent me. The Father who sent me, he himself speaks on my behalf. You've never heard his voice, and you've never seen what he looks like,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and you don't accept what he says, because you don't trust in the one he sent.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You search the Scriptures because you think that through them you'll gain eternal life. But the evidence they give is in support of me!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And yet you don't want to come to me so that you might live.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I'm not looking for human approval
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: —I know you, and that you don't have God's love in you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I've come to represent my Father, and you won't accept me; but if someone comes representing themselves, then you accept them!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How can you trust in me when you look for praise from one another and yet you don't look for praise from the one true God?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But don't think I will be making accusations about you to the Father. It's Moses who is accusing you, the one in whom you place such confidence.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For if you really trusted Moses you would trust in me, because he wrote about me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But since you don't trust what he said, why would you trust what I say?
segments.speaker: Jesus
